paper_map.title: An Ultrastrong and Ductile Duplex Lightweight Steel with Dual-Nanoprecipitation
paper_map.abstract_summary: A duplex lightweight steel Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni (wt.%) was designed, consisting of austenite and B2 phases with dual-nanoprecipitation of L'1_2-type long-range ordered domains and VC carbides within austenite. The steel exhibits an ultrahigh yield strength of 1316 MPa, tensile strength of 1458 MPa, and total elongation of 11.7%. High strength originates from hetero-deformation induced strengthening, solid solution strengthening, and precipitation strengthening; excellent ductility is attributed to substantial dislocation accumulation and HDI hardening at phase boundaries.
paper_map.research_objective: To simultaneously achieve ultrahigh strength and good ductility in a lightweight steel by combining LRO and VC precipitation-strengthened austenite with a hard B2 phase to create a heterogeneous duplex structure that exploits multiple strengthening mechanisms.
material_systems: Precipitates: L'1_2-type long-range ordered domains, VC carbides
main_process_variables: Hot rolling to 3 mm then water quenching
Cold rolling with 50% total reduction (single-pass ≤5%) to 1.5 mm
Solution treatment at 900 °C for 15 min followed by water quenching
expected_information_axis: microstructural parameters (grain size, phase fraction, precipitate size/distribution)
tensile properties (YS, UTS, TEL, work-hardening stages)
strengthening contributions (HDI, solid solution, dislocation, precipitation)

key_sections.heading: 1. Introduction
key_sections.role: Reviews high-Mn lightweight steels, issues with κ-carbides, benefits of LRO domains and VC precipitation, and heterogeneous structure strategy; states the research aim.

key_sections.heading: 2. Materials and Methods
key_sections.role: Describes alloy processing (melting, homogenization, hot/cold rolling, heat treatment) and characterization methods.

key_sections.heading: 3.1. Initial Microstructures
key_sections.role: Characterizes the as-processed duplex microstructure including austenite, B2, LRO domains, and VC nanoprecipitates.

key_sections.heading: 3.2. Mechanical Properties
key_sections.role: Presents tensile stress-strain curves, yield strength, tensile strength, total elongation, and work-hardening rate analysis.

key_sections.heading: 3.3. Evolution of Microstructures During Deformation
key_sections.role: Investigates dislocation structures, GND distributions, and precipitate-dislocation interactions at various strains.

key_sections.heading: 4.1. Multiple Strengthening Mechanisms
key_sections.role: Calculates and discusses contributions of HDI stress, solid solution strengthening, dislocation strengthening, and LRO/VC precipitation strengthening to yield strength.

key_sections.heading: 4.2. Strain Hardening Mechanisms Responsible for Tensile Ductility
key_sections.role: Explains the three work-hardening stages in terms of dislocation storage, dynamic recovery, and HDI hardening at austenite/B2 interfaces.

key_sections.heading: 5. Conclusions
key_sections.role: Summarizes the microstructure, mechanical performance, strengthening mechanisms, and strain hardening characteristics.
